title: primula auriculata subsp. tournefortii
intro: Primula auriculata subsp. tournefortii, commonly known as the Auricula, is a species of flowering plant in the family Primulaceae, native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Primula auriculata subsp. tournefortii include its potential to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its ability to aid in the treatment of digestive problems. The plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
